title: The Stopped Chronometer
subtitle: Chapter 02 · The Wider Fractures
introduction: A watchmaker's workshop is filled with brass instruments under oil-lamp light. Focus on the bench balance wheel.
description: A watchmaker's workshop is filled with brass instruments under oil-lamp light. Focus on the bench balance wheel.
observation: A marine chronometer has stopped, but its brass balance wheel suddenly oscillates and spins wildly.
reconstruction: Carry the steel mainspring, the escape wheel gear, and the chronometer face.
attunements: The steel mainspring remains fully wound and locked in stasis.
The escapement gear teeth show zero wear or escape friction.
The second hand moves in reverse, counting future ticks.
revelation:
What was wrong: the clockwork gathered momentum while the spring remained locked in stasis.

Why it mattered: the chronometer was not measuring the present time. It was measuring the ticking countdown of the watchmaker's final day.

anomaly_definition.misstep_text: Not that. Watch the brass balance wheel on the workbench.
anomaly_definition.success_text: Anomalous kinetic spin detected. The wheels turn with no mainspring energy.

capture_window.guidance_text: HOLD WHEN THE BALANCE WHEEL ACCELERATES.

evidence_nodes.relevance: THE STEEL MAINSPRING REMAINS LOCKED. It stands fully coiled, retaining its potential energy while gears spin.
THE GEAR TEETH SHOW NO ESCAPE WEAR. The escapement has not yet released the pallet fork.
THE SECOND HAND COUNTS IN REVERSE. The chronometer measures a temporal backward drift.
